puzzle.title: The Test of Wealth and Gratitude
puzzle.intro: A man of immense fortune was put to the ultimate test. Would his wealth lead him to arrogance and forgetfulness of his Lord, or would he remain steadfast in gratitude? Unravel the clues to discover this individual's identity and the nature of his divine trial.
clues: He was blessed with abundance beyond measure.
His prosperity was a test of his faith and humility.
He was warned about the dangers of being consumed by worldly possessions.
He attributed his success solely to his own knowledge and abilities, forgetting the source of his blessings.
An orchard was central to his trial, representing his abundant provision.
His once-flourishing possessions were reduced to barrenness overnight as a consequence of his arrogance.

outcome.label: Outcome
options: He was granted even greater wealth
His wealth was destroyed, and he was left in regret
outcome.answer: His wealth was destroyed, and he was left in regret

puzzle.verse: And he regretted what he had spent on it after it had collapsed upon its trellises and said, "Oh, I wish I had not associated anyone with my Lord." (Al-Kahf:42)
puzzle.arabic: وَأُحِيطَ بِثَمَرِهِۦ فَأَصْبَحَ يُقَلِّبُ كَفَّيْهِ عَلَىٰ مَآ أَنفَقَ فِيهَا وَهِىَ خَاوِيَةٌ عَلَىٰ عُرُوشِهَا وَيَقُولُ يَٰلَيْتَنِى لَمْ أُشْرِكْ بِرَبِّىٓ أَحَدًا
